_comment: CONFORMANCE FIXTURES for the qeli:// share link — the ONE source of truth that all
four independent parsers are checked against (Rust, Kotlin/Android, C#/desktop,
WHY: the link format is implemented four times, from scratch, in four languages. Every
field is therefore four chances to disagree, and the failure mode is silent — the link
imports 'successfully' on one platform with a field dropped or defaulted, and the user
gets a profile that cannot connect (or, worse, connects with weaker settings than the
link asked for). Two real examples already found this way: Android EMITTED `mtu` but had
no case to parse it back, and an omitted `bind_static` silently re-defaulted to ON,
demanding a key the link never carried.
HOW TO USE: each platform has a test that reads this file, parses `uri`, and asserts
every field in `expect`. A field absent from `expect` is not asserted (platforms differ
in which extras they model). `null` means 'absent / not set', NOT the empty string.
WHEN CHANGING THE LINK FORMAT: add the case HERE first, then make the four parsers
agree. A case that only one implementation passes is the bug this file exists to catch.

cases.name: minimal-tcp-faketls
cases.why: The shortest legal link: only the mandatory fields.

cases.name: pinned-key-and-sni
cases.why: A pinned server identity (64 hex) plus an explicit SNI.

cases.name: reality-with-short-id
cases.why: reality-tls needs BOTH the key and the short_id; dropping rsid silently downgrades the profile to a plain one that the server will not answer.

cases.name: udp-quic
cases.why: quic=1 is mandatory for a udp+quic profile — without it the client sends plain UDP and a quic-mode server never replies.

cases.name: obfs-with-key
cases.why: obfs mode carries a pre-shared key; the key must survive percent-decoding intact.

cases.name: awg-junk-params
cases.why: AmneziaWG junk must match the server exactly; a dropped jc/jmin/jmax means the peer discards the handshake.

cases.name: ipv6-literal-host
cases.why: A bracketed IPv6 authority: the colons inside the address must not be read as the port separator.

cases.name: percent-encoded-credentials
cases.why: Passwords legitimately contain @ : / ? # — all of which are URI syntax. Mis-decoding here authenticates with the wrong password and looks like a server-side auth failure.

cases.name: unknown-params-ignored
cases.why: Forward compatibility: a link from a newer version must still import, ignoring what this build does not know.

reject.name: no-port
reject.why: The port is mandatory — guessing 443 would silently connect somewhere the link never specified.

reject.name: port-out-of-range
reject.why: 65536 does not fit a u16; accepting it produced a profile that failed later with an opaque socket error.

reject.name: ipv6-without-port
reject.why: A bracketed host with no port must not be parsed as host+port.
